info.topictitle: B2.2.1 Drawings for feedback and communication

markdown:
### The Role of Drawings in Design


**Drawings** are essential tools in design, serving multiple purposes:

1. **Communication**: They convey ideas clearly to stakeholders, including clients, engineers, and manufacturers.
2. **Feedback**: Drawings enable designers to receive input and make informed decisions.
3. **Documentation**: They provide a record of the design process, ensuring consistency and accuracy.

<callout type="note">Drawings are not just artistic representations, they are precise tools that bridge the gap between ideas and reality.</callout>

<callout type="note">**Types of Drawings in Design**

* **2D Drawings**: Show specific views or details (e.g. front, side, top)
* **3D Models**: Visualise the full form and structure of a product</callout>

### Drawing Types – for Communication


<callout type="note">While these drawing types were introduced in **A2.2.2** (for manufacture), here they support **idea sharing** and **concept refinement.**</callout>

| Type | Used to... |
| --- | --- |
| Isometric View | Show overall form and appearance in 3D |
| Orthographic Views | Communicate size and layout in multiple angles |
| Exploded View | Clarify how parts fit together for clearer explanation |
| Assembly Drawing | Show relationships between parts or components |
| 3D CAD Models | Offer interactive or rendered visuals for clarity |

<callout type="note">See A2.2.2 for guidance on dimensions, scale, and technical accuracy.</callout>

<callout type="tip">Don't rely on one drawing type alone, combine **2D and 3D views**, labels, and annotations to support your explanation.</callout>
